feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Linking between Installment Invoice and its payment
entry.content:
We are creating a custom report for Customer receivable

Here we have a condition that on billing document when we take payment term as installment system splits the bill 

Next we are adjusting a payment against installment 

but we are not able to find any relation between the payment and installment

Request you to help us know how we can create a link between installment and payment in custom CDS so that we can show exactly against which installment payment is received.

entry.title: Mass Copy of BOM and Routing to New Plant in S/4HANA Public Cloud (Bulk ~500 Materials)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working on extending existing materials to a new plant in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have around 500 finished materials for which the following already exists in the old plant:

  • Bill of Material (BOM)
  • Routing

Now, I want to replicate the same setup for a new plant, ensuring:

  • Same BOM structure (header + items + alternatives if any)
  • Same Routing (operations, new work centers.)

Is there any standard way in Public Cloud to:

  1. Copy BOMs from one plant to another in bulk
  2. Copy Routings from one plant to another in bulk

What I Checked So Far

  • Migration object (BOM / Routing) → seems possible, but requires extraction + upload again
  • Fiori apps → mostly support single material

entry.title: budget on Fixed Asset Purchasing in sap public cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

The on-premise version provides standard SAP features that manage budget availability for Fixed Asset Purchasing (Balance Sheet, account assignment A). I am wondering whether this capability is also enabled in the SAP public cloud?

Please be aware that I am referring to the end-to-end functionality outlined below:

  1. Develop statistical WBS to facilitate effective budget availability control.
  2. Upload budget amount for the combination of WBS and BS acquisition account for Asset.
  3. Create a final asset that contains in master data the above wbs.
  4. Create PO for purchasing assets with account assignment A.
  5. Post the asset acquisition.

Is budget availability for Fixed Asset Purchasing supported in SAP Public Cloud?

Best regards, 

Ioanna

entry.title: WM Inbound Putaway Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, I would like to clarify a scenario in inbound putaway in public cloud:

I have defined a pallet with a capacity of 1000 kg, and I have enabled the setting “Do Not Split Putaway” during the putaway process.

However, in a situation where 3 pallets arrive at the same time, I would like to understand:

  1. How does the system handle multiple pallets when “Do Not Split Putaway” is active?
  2. What is the best way to handle this scenario properly — should we use Handling Unit (HU) Management so that each pallet is processed individually?
  3. Are there recommended configurations (e.g., HU type, storage type settings, capacity check) to ensure each pallet is put away correctly without violating the “Do Not Split Putaway” rule?

Appreciate your guidance on best practice for this setup. Thank you.

entry.title: Unable to Find “Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting-Specific” in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently working on Asset Accounting configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

However, we are unable to locate the configuration step “Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting-Specific” in the system.

entry.title: Mass change in the Purchase Scheduling Agreement Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

I would like to raise a ticket regarding a mass change requirement in the Purchase scheduling agreement schedule lines within the public cloud environment. The issue involves updating multiple schedule lines simultaneously, and we are seeking guidance or support on how to execute this efficiently.

Please advise on the process or provide assistance in implementing this change.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost center for small price difference GL/ round off GL
entry.content:
Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost center for small price difference GL/ round off GL while doing MIRO  - App Create Supplier Invoice Advanced 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to map ECC SPRO/IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SSCUIs - Looking for pract
entry.content:
Body:
Hi Community,
I'm working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation project where we are migrating from ECC 6.0. During the Fit-to-Standard (F2S) workshops, I need to create mapping documentation that shows:

ECC side: SPRO path / IMG Activity ID / Transaction Code
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition side: SSCUI ID / Fiori App name / Configuration path

My Goal:
Create a comprehensive mapping table for FI module scope items (e.g., Accounts Receivable, Credit Management, General Ledger, etc.) to support:

Gap analysis documentation
BDCQ (Business-Driven Configuration Questionnaire) creation
Knowledge transfer from ECC consultants to Cloud consultants

What I've found so far:

SAP Notes mention "Configuration Activity.xlsm (SAP Customer)" in Roadmap Viewer
Some community posts reference the CUS_IMGACH table for reverse lookup
But I haven't found a ready-made, publicly available mapping document

My Questions:
1. Does such a mapping document exist?

Is there an official SAP document/spreadsheet that maps ECC IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud SSCUIs?
Or is "Configuration Activity.xlsm" the closest thing available?

2. If I need to use Configuration Activity.xlsm:

Step 1: Where exactly in Roadmap Viewer do I download it? (Which Roadmap? Which section?)
Step 2: Which sheet(s) should I look at? (I heard "IMG_Act BusinessCatalog Match" sheet exists)
Step 3: How do I search/filter to find SSCUI for a specific ECC IMG Activity or SPRO path?
Step 4: Can I export/pivot this data to create my own mapping table?

3. Alternative approaches:

Are there any SAP Help Portal pages that document SSCUI↔IMG relationships by module (FI/CO/SD/MM)?
Can I use Best Practice Explorer to find this mapping?
Should I check CBC (Central Business Configuration) documentation instead?
Any community-maintained spreadsheets or GitHub repos with this mapping?

4. Specific example:

ECC: SPRO → Financial Accounting → Accounts Receivable → Business Transactions → Outgoing Payments → Automatic Outgoing Payments → Payment Methods per Company Code
What is the equivalent SSCUI ID / Fiori App in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Background:
I'm an SAP FI consultant with strong ECC/on-premise experience but new to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I can navigate SPRO blindfolded, but the Fiori-based configuration approach is still new to me.
Any concrete, actionable guidance with screenshots or step-by-step instructions would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance for your help!
Best regards

entry.title: In Manage Production Orders, the column “Order Text” remains empty
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and noticed that the column “Order Text” in the app Manage Production Orders remains empty, even though I have maintained header text / long text / short text on the production order.

Could you please clarify which exact field or text source is used to fill this column?
Is this column linked to a specific order description field rather than the maintained production order texts?

Additionally, is there any standard way in the capacity planning apps to display such production order long texts or shift notes directly for the orders?

Kind regards,

Jana

entry.title: Order confirmation CDS View
entry.content:
1.For the Order Confirmation output print from Sales order, the “Tax Classification for Customer 1” field is available in CDS view I_SalesDocument, but the data/value is not available in CDS view I_SalesDocument.

2. From which CDS view can we retrieve the Tax Code, Sales office mobile phone number and Payment Due Date.

All the above-mentioned data, such as Tax Classification Customer 1 and Tax Code, are available in Sales Order 268 on the DEV system. The Sales Office mobile phone number is maintained in the master data and the Payment Due Date from which CDS view we can retrieve. Please let us know the update.

entry.title: Payment methods are not inherited from selected open items in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are using the “Clear Incoming Payments” app to clear open items.

When we select original open items for offsetting/clearing, we expected some payment-related fields from the selected original line items to be inherited into the clearing/payment document. However, the following three fields are not inherited from the selected open items:

  • Payment Method
  • Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  • Cash Discount Days 1

Our main question is:

Is there any standard configuration, app setting, BAdI, substitution, or other enhancement option that allows these three fields to be inherited from the selected original open items during clearing in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app?

We would like to know whether SAP standard behavior intentionally does not copy these fields during clearing, or whether there is a way to control the field transfer.

The important point is that we are not simply asking why the fields are blank.
We specifically want to know whether there is any possible solution to make the following fields inherit from the selected source open item:

  1. Payment Method
  2. Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  3. Cash Discount Days 1

If this is standard behavior and these fields cannot be inherited in this app, we would also appreciate confirmation and any recommended workaround.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Missing “Selection Parameters” in PROD for Fiori App
entry.content:
Body:

Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

In the Fiori application (Schedule Accounting Data Changes / related app), we can see the “Selection Parameters” section in the DEV / configuration environment. However, in the PROD system, this section is missing.

In PROD, only the execution screen is displayed, and it is not possible to maintain or view selection parameters.

We would like to confirm the following points:

  • Is this expected standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • If yes, where are these selection parameters stored and maintain

entry.title: change of Payment format for sales(customer incoming payments)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 

As we wanted to change payment format for customer payments where excatly we change the format in the sap public cloud .when we upload new bank statements it should allow new format that is required to accept the payments

entry.title: Verification of Purchasing Group determination logic for manual PR/PO (Reference: KBA 3728011)
entry.content:
When creating a Purchase Requisition (PR) or Purchase Order (PO) manually in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system automatically proposes Purchasing Group "001" from the Material Master, instead of "002" from the Purchasing Info Record.
what is the standard behavior for manual PRs and POs is for the Purchasing Group from the Material Master to take priority.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: I want to know how to resolve real-time revenue errors.
entry.content:
An error occurs stating that real-time posting is not supported when assigning accounts to context assets. I would like to know how to resolve this. 

While reprocessing with real-time revenue recognition allows revenue recognition to run, this error appears every month, so I want to resolve it.

Work procedure

1.Register client projects based on their progress. 

2. Register the account category while linking it to the customer project.

3.Register fixed assets

4.Register a supplier invoice.

5.In the supplier invoice approval workflow, the approver edits the details of the supplier invoice. The edit involves setting fixed assets to the details of the supplier invoice for the order registered in the project's account assignment.

6.Complete the supplier invoice approval flow.

7.Perform revenue recognition

entry.title: Impact Analysis (Change Record) – “Key is invalid” error for BOM in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 260
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community 🙏

We are working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 and implementing Impact Analysis within Change Records (Engineering Change Management).  We are facing an issue when trying to execute an impact analysis on a BOM object.

Issue
When launching Impact Analysis from a Change Record with a BOM item, we receive the following error:
Key is invalid. Check the UI Field Mapping Customization.
or sometimes:
Object <Plant/Material/Alt> does not exist or you do not have the requisite authorization

Context
We are trying to perform impact analysis for a Bill of Material (BOM) using:

  • Object: BOM (S03)
  • CDS View: A_BILLOFMATERIAL_1

The Change Record contains a BOM with:

  • Material
  • Plant
  • BOM Usage (Production / Engineering)
  • Alternative BOM

Configuration done (CBC)

  • Impact Modeler Object Configuration
    • Object: BOM (S03)
    • CDS View: A_BILLOFMATERIAL_1
  • Analyzer Field Mapping

APPS "Manage Impact Analysis Models":

APPS "Impact Analysis"

APPS "Change Record":

 

Questions

  1. Does Impact Analysis with CDS A_BILLOFMATERIAL_1 require a technical BOM identifier (BillOfMaterial UUID) instead of the functional key (Material + Plant + Usage + Alternative)?
  2. Is there any standard limitation on BOM Usage (e.g. Engineering BOM not supported)?
  3. Is additional configuration required to map the BOM key correctly between Change Record UI and CDS view?
  4. Are there known issues or OSS notes related to this error in version 2602?

entry.title: Packing Instruction - Reference Material
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently testing packing instructions using a reference material.

According to the SAP documentation, a reference material can be used to define a packing logic that is then reused by other materials assigned to it in the material master. Based on this, my expectation is that maintaining the packing instruction determination only for the reference material should be sufficient.

In my setup:

  • I created the packing instruction using the reference material
  • I maintained condition records (POF1) for 0IBD and 0GRV using the reference material
  • I assigned this reference material in the material master of other products that should follow the same packing logic

However, the system only determines the packing instruction when I maintain the condition records directly for each individual material. The determination is not triggered based on the reference material.

Is it expected behavior that packing instruction determination (POF1) must still be maintained individually for each material, even when a reference material is assigned?

Or is there any additional configuration required to enable determination via reference material as described in the documentation?

My understanding was that the reference material should allow reusing packing instructions without needing to maintain condition records per material.

entry.title: Cost Center Automatically Populated in Service Entry Sheet Despite Only WBS Element Being Entered
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud related to Service Entry Sheet (SES) postings and would like to understand the source of automatic Cost Center determination.

Scenario:

  • During Service Entry Sheet posting, the user is entering only a WBS Element.
  • No Cost Center is manually entered by the user.
  • However, the system is automatically populating a Cost Center in the SES line item.

We tried analyzing the source of this Cost Center and checked the following:

  1. The Cost Center assigned to the WBS Element used in the posting.
    • The Cost Center maintained in the WBS Element is different from the Cost Center appearing in the SES posting.
  2. OKB9 default account assignment.
    • We checked whether the G/L account used in the SES has any Cost Center maintained in OKB9.
    • No matching Cost Center assignment was found there as well.

Since the Cost Center appearing in the SES does not match either:

  • the WBS master data assignment, or
  • the OKB9 default assignment,

we are unable to identify from where exactly the system is deriving this Cost Center.

We would like to understand:

  1. What are the other possible sources or determination logic for automatic Cost Center derivation in SES postings?
  2. Are there any standard tables, derivation rules, account assignment settings, substitution logic, or integration points that should be checked?
  3. Has anyone faced a similar issue where the system derives a different Cost Center automatically during SES posting?

Any guidance on additional checks or troubleshooting steps would be highly appreciated.

Thank you.

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: 2PD – Provision for Doubtful Receivables not updating for new items in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I’m currently working on the 2PD – Provisioning of Overdue Receivables scope item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I’ve encountered a behavior that I’d like to confirm with the community. 

 

To give you a bit more context: 

  • Transfer Posting job (Step 1) and batch sessions work correctly:
    • Overdue receivables are transferred to Special G/L indicator E (doubtful receivables) 
  • Customer master data is correctly maintained:
    • Company code view available 
    • Value Adjustment Key assigned (R1) 
  • Provision matrix is configured (based on ageing buckets and percentages) 
  • Valuation area is correctly maintained (FR) 

 

The issue observed is: 

When running the “Provisions for Doubtful Receivables” job (Step 2): 

  • Only one old receivable is returned in the output 
  • The same item appears repeatedly across runs 
  • For this item:  
    • “Until Provision” = 0 
    • Posting amount = 0
  • New overdue receivables (which were correctly picked up by Step 1) are not considered in Step 2 

 

My question is: 

  • Why are newly transferred doubtful receivables (Step 1) not appearing in Step 2? 

Even though: 

  • They are overdue 
  • They are assigned to Special G/L E 
  • They fall into valid ageing buckets 

 

Thank you in advance for your insights! 
Happy to provide more details if needed

entry.title: Custom report : Stock In‑Out & Balance Report with Transaction currency
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

(Product: S/4HANA Public Cloud)

Our client use Company Code Currency A, but daily transactions are settled in Currency B or C.
 
Inventory reports exported from SAP show inventory values calculated by moving average price under Company Code Currency A.
 
The client requires all inventory reports to be displayed based on transaction currency B or C. Purchasing, sales and inventory data should all be shown in transaction currency B or C.
 
We plan to implemente via Custom CDS View + Custom Query, we can obtain Material Documents items of all movement types, their posting dates, materials and quantities from view: 
I_MaterialDocumentItem_2.
 
For GR material document with movement type 101, we can get the Document Currency(transaction currency) and Net Price Amount from view: I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 by linking PO and PO Item within the material document item.
 
The current challenge is to calculate the material moving average price in transaction currency without exchange rate conversion.
 
Since 'Out Amount', 'opening inventory amount' and 'closing inventory amount' all need to be calculated using the moving average price under transaction currency, how to implement step‑by‑step rolling calculation of moving average price?
 
and how to get the Opening Inventory Qty ? 
 

thanks a lot.

Best regards.
